---
title: "Validation1_tissue cryopreservation proteomics test"
output: html_document
date: "2025-04-09"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
# Load necessary library
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(pheatmap)
library(clusterProfiler)
library(enrichplot)
library(BioNet)
library(stringr)
library(pcaMethods)
library(DESeq2)
library(ggpubr)
library(org.Mm.eg.db)
library(VIM)
library(AnnotationDbi)
library(ggrepel)
```

```{r read in intensity values from n3 sheet, echo = TRUE}

intensity_raw <- read_xlsx("/path-to-your-file/data.xlsx", sheet = 1) %>%
  filter(`Protein FDR Confidence: Combined` != "Checked")%>%
  dplyr::select(4:5, 22:31)

colnames(intensity_raw) <- c("Proteins","Description","Control_1","Cryo_1","Control_2","Cryo_2", "Control_3", "Cryo_3","Control_4", "Cryo_4", "Control_5", "Cryo_5")

# check the column data type
# convert the selected column from character or factor types to numeric type

sapply(intensity_raw, class)

intensity_raw <- intensity_raw %>%
  dplyr::mutate(across(c(Control_1,Cryo_1,Control_2,Cryo_2,Control_3,Cryo_3,Control_4,Cryo_4,Control_5, Cryo_5), as.numeric))

sapply(intensity_raw, class)

# Remove rows where all values are NA
intensity_raw <- intensity_raw %>%
  dplyr::filter(rowSums(!is.na(dplyr::select(., where(is.numeric)))) > 0)

# Remove rows with fewer than 2 non-NA values in each group
intensity_raw <- intensity_raw %>%
  dplyr::filter(
    rowSums(!is.na(dplyr::select(., starts_with("Control_")))) >= 2 &
    rowSums(!is.na(dplyr::select(., starts_with("Cryo_")))) >= 2
  )


```

```{r gene mapping using uniprot file, echo = TRUE}

uniprot_ids <- read_xlsx("path-to-data/uniprotkb_AND_model_organism_10090_AND_2024_10_07.xlsx")%>%
  dplyr::select(Entry, `Gene Names`)%>%
  dplyr::rename("Proteins"="Entry")

intensity_raw_map <- intensity_raw %>%
  left_join(.,uniprot_ids, by = "Proteins")%>%
  mutate(`Gene Names` = gsub(" .*","",`Gene Names`))%>%
  dplyr::select(Proteins,`Gene Names`,3:12)%>%
  dplyr::rename("Genes"="Gene Names")

intensity_raw_map$Genes <- make.unique(intensity_raw_map$Genes)
  
intensity_raw <- intensity_raw_map
```

```{r data normalization, echo = TRUE}
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(vsn)
library(limma)

# Ensure all gene names in the 'Genes' column are unique
numeric_cols <- intensity_raw %>% 
  dplyr::select(-c(Proteins)) %>% 
  mutate(Genes = ifelse(is.na(Genes) | Genes == "", "NA", Genes)) %>% 
  column_to_rownames(var = "Genes") %>%  # Set 'Genes' as row names
  mutate(across(starts_with("Control_") | starts_with("Cryo_"), as.numeric))  # Convert specified columns to numeric

# Check if numeric conversion was successful
str(numeric_cols)

# Assuming `numeric_data` is your data matrix
vsn_data <- normalizeVSN(as.matrix(numeric_cols))

# Verify fit using meanSdPlot
meanSdPlot(vsn_data)

# Convert to a data frame for ggplot2
normalized_df <- as.data.frame(vsn_data)
```

```{r data imputation, echo = TRUE}
# Impute missing values from a normal distribution
impute_normal <- function(x) {
  missing <- is.na(x)
  n_missing <- sum(missing)
  if (n_missing > 0) {
    imputed_values <- rnorm(n_missing, mean(x, na.rm = TRUE) - 1.8 * sd(x, na.rm = TRUE), 0.3 * sd(x, na.rm = TRUE))
    x[missing] <- imputed_values
  }
  return(x)
}

# Impute zero values
intensity_imputed <- normalized_df %>%
  mutate(across(starts_with("Control_") | starts_with("Cryo_") , impute_normal))

```

PCA plot
```{r Batch correction and PCA, echo=TRUE}
# Perform PCA
# Remove Protein_ID column for PCA

pca_data <- intensity_imputed 

# Perform PCA
# Perform PCA on the log-transformed data
pca_results <- pca(t(pca_data), method = "svd")

# Extract PCA scores
pca_scores <- as.data.frame(scores(pca_results))
pca_scores$Sample <- rownames(pca_scores)

#Prepare pheno_dat
pheno_dat <- as.data.frame(rownames(pca_scores)) %>%
  dplyr::rename("Sample" = "rownames(pca_scores)") %>%
  mutate(Group = case_when(
    grepl("Control", Sample) ~ "Control",
    grepl("Cryo", Sample) ~ "Cryo",
    TRUE ~ "Unknown"
  )) %>%
  left_join(pca_scores, by = "Sample") %>%
  mutate(batch =ifelse(Sample %in% c("Control_1", "Cryo_1", "Control_2", "Cryo_2"),"batch_1",""))%>%
  mutate(batch =ifelse(Sample %in% c("Control_3","Cryo_3","Control_4", "Cryo_4", "Control_5", "Cryo_5"),"batch_2",batch))

```

```{r Continue batch correction and PCA, echo = TRUE}
library(sva)
library(ggrepel)

# Convert normalized_df to a numeric matrix 
normalized_matrix <- as.matrix(intensity_imputed)

# Ensure that batch is a factor in pheno_dat
pheno_dat$batch <- as.factor(pheno_dat$batch)
pheno_dat$Group <- as.factor(pheno_dat$Group)

# Apply ComBat to correct for batch effects
# Create a design matrix
mod <- model.matrix(~ Group, data = pheno_dat)

# Apply ComBat for batch correction
batch_corrected <- ComBat(dat = normalized_matrix,
                           batch = pheno_dat$batch, 
                           mod = mod,              
                           par.prior = TRUE, 
                           prior.plots = FALSE)    

batch_corrected <- as.data.frame(batch_corrected)

# Perform PCA
pca_result <- pca(t(batch_corrected), method = "svd")

# Extract PCA scores
pca_batch_correct <- as.data.frame(scores(pca_result))
pca_batch_correct$Sample <- rownames(pca_batch_correct)


pheno_dat <- pheno_dat %>%
  left_join(pca_batch_correct, by = "Sample") 


# Plot PCA results
pca_group <- ggplot(pheno_dat, aes(x = PC1.y, y = PC2.y, color = Group)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  # ADD ggforce's ellipses
  #ggforce::geom_mark_ellipse(aes(color = batch))+
  labs(title = "PCA by group", x = "PC1", y = "PC2") +
  theme_bw()

pca_group


pca_sample <- ggplot(pheno_dat, aes(x = PC1.y, y = PC2.y, color = Sample, shape = Group)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  labs(title = "PCA by sample and treatment", x = "PC1", y = "PC2") +
  theme_bw()

pca_sample

pca_sample <- ggplot(pheno_dat, aes(x = PC1.y, y = PC2.y, color = Group, shape = batch)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  labs(title = "PCA by batch and group", x = "PC1", y = "PC2") +
  theme_bw()

pca_sample

pca_sample <- ggplot(pheno_dat, aes(x = PC1.y, y = PC2.y, color = Group)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = Sample), size = 4, max.overlaps = Inf) +
  labs(title = "PCA by batch and group", x = "PC1", y = "PC2") +
  theme_bw()

pca_sample
```


```{r perform pearson correlation plot, echo = TRUE}
library(pheatmap)
library(dplyr)

# Transpose intensity matrix so rows = samples, columns = proteins
expr_t <- t(intensity_imputed)

# Make sure it's a numeric matrix
expr_matrix <- as.matrix(expr_t)

# Compute sample-to-sample Pearson correlation
cor_matrix <- cor(t(expr_matrix), method = "pearson", use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

# Make an annotation data frame for sample groups
sample_annotation <- pheno_dat %>%
  column_to_rownames("Sample") %>%   # Match rownames with colnames of cor_matrix
  dplyr::select(Group)

# Plot with group annotations
pheatmap(cor_matrix,
         annotation_col = sample_annotation,
         annotation_row = sample_annotation,
         clustering_distance_rows = "correlation",
         clustering_distance_cols = "correlation",
         main = "Sample-to-Sample Pearson Correlation",
         fontsize = 10,
         display_numbers = FALSE,
         number_format = "%.2f")
```

```{r perform pearson correlation plot with batch correction, echo = TRUE}
library(pheatmap)
library(dplyr)

# Transpose intensity matrix so rows = samples, columns = proteins
expr_t <- t(batch_corrected)

# Make sure it's a numeric matrix
expr_matrix <- as.matrix(expr_t)

# Compute sample-to-sample Pearson correlation
cor_matrix <- cor(t(expr_matrix), method = "pearson", use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

# Make an annotation data frame for sample groups
sample_annotation <- pheno_dat %>%
  column_to_rownames("Sample") %>%   # Match rownames with colnames of cor_matrix
  dplyr::select(Group)

#save the image 
png("path-to-your-directory/correlation.png",
    width = 6,
    height = 4,
    units = "in",
    res = 600)

# Plot with group annotations
pheatmap(cor_matrix,
         annotation_col = sample_annotation,
         annotation_row = sample_annotation,
         color= colorRampPalette(c("navy", "white", "firebrick3"))(20),
         clustering_distance_rows = "correlation",
         clustering_distance_cols = "correlation",
         main = "Sample-to-Sample Pearson Correlation",
         fontsize = 10,
         display_numbers = FALSE,
         number_format = "%.2f")

dev.off()
```

```{r perform pearson correlationship plot by groups, echo = TRUE}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# Identify Control and Cryopreservation columns
control_cols <- grep("^Control_", colnames(batch_corrected), value = TRUE)
cryo_cols <- grep("^Cryo_", colnames(batch_corrected), value = TRUE)

# Calculate group-wise average expression per protein
group_means_log2 <- batch_corrected %>%
  mutate(Control_mean = rowMeans(dplyr::select(., all_of(control_cols)), na.rm = TRUE),
         Cryo_mean = rowMeans(dplyr::select(., all_of(cryo_cols)), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  dplyr::select(Control_mean, Cryo_mean) %>%
  mutate(Protein = rownames(batch_corrected))

# Calculate correlation and n
cor_value <- cor(group_means_log2$Control_mean, group_means_log2$Cryo_mean, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
n_proteins <- nrow(group_means_log2)

# Create plot with annotation
ggplot(group_means_log2, aes(x = Control_mean, y = Cryo_mean)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  coord_equal() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Protein Correlation: Control vs. Cryo (log2-transformed)",
       x = "log2(Average Expression in Control)",
       y = "log2(Average Expression in Cryo)") +
  annotate("text",
           x = min(group_means_log2$Control_mean) + 1,
           y = max(group_means_log2$Cryo_mean) - 1,
           label = paste0("n = ", n_proteins, "\nR = ", round(cor_value, 3)),
           hjust = 0, vjust = 1, size = 5, fontface = "italic")

```


```{r perform coefficients of variation plot Cryopreservation group, echo = TRUE}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpointdensity)
library(viridis)

# Get Cryopreservation sample columns
log2_data <- intensity_raw
log2_data[ , -(1:2)] <- log2(log2_data[ ,-(1:2)])
cryo_cols <- grep("^Cryo_", colnames(log2_data), value = TRUE)

# Calculate mean and CV for Cryo samples only
cv_cryo_plot <- log2_data %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    # Mean expression across Cryo samples
    mean_log2_cryo = mean(c_across(all_of(cryo_cols)), na.rm = TRUE),
    
    # Coefficient of variation across Cryo samples
    cv_cryo = sd(c_across(all_of(cryo_cols)), na.rm = TRUE) / 
                  mean(c_across(all_of(cryo_cols)), na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  dplyr::select(mean_log2_cryo, cv_cryo) %>%
  mutate(Protein = rownames(log2_data))

# Plot with density-colored dots
ggplot(cv_cryo_plot, aes(x = mean_log2_cryo, y = cv_cryo)) +
  geom_pointdensity(adjust = 1) +
  scale_color_viridis(name = "Point Density", option = "plasma") +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "CV vs Mean log2 Intensity in Cryo Group",
    x = "Mean log2 Intensity (Cryo)",
    y = "Coefficient of Variation"
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 11)
  )
```


```{r perform coefficients of variation plot control group, echo = TRUE}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpointdensity)
library(viridis)


# Get control sample columns
control_cols <- grep("^Control_", colnames(intensity_imputed), value = TRUE)

# Calculate mean and CV for control samples only
cv_control_plot <- intensity_imputed %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    # Mean expression across control samples
    mean_log2_control = mean(c_across(all_of(control_cols)), na.rm = TRUE),
    
    # Coefficient of Variation across control samples
    cv_control = sd(c_across(all_of(control_cols)), na.rm = TRUE) / 
                  mean(c_across(all_of(control_cols)), na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  dplyr::select(mean_log2_control, cv_control) %>%
  mutate(Protein = rownames(intensity_imputed))

# Plot with density-colored dots
ggplot(cv_control_plot, aes(x = mean_log2_control, y = cv_control)) +
  geom_pointdensity(adjust = 1) +
  scale_color_viridis(name = "Point Density", option = "plasma") +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "CV vs Mean log2 Intensity in Control Group",
    x = "Mean log2 Intensity (Control)",
    y = "Coefficient of Variation"
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 11)
  )

```
